behavior_summary: Continuously drives gray as the Gray-code equivalent of the 10-bit binary input: gray[9] mirrors bin[9], and each lower bit gray[i] for i=0..8 equals bin[i+1] XOR bin[i], equivalent to gray = bin ^ (bin >> 1).
edge_cases: Purely combinational: gray updates immediately with any change on bin; no storage or timing behavior.
No overflow/underflow handling is needed because conversion is bitwise; the operation naturally maps all 10-bit values 1:1 to Gray code.
